{{short description|Transcription factor gene of the regulatory factor X family}}
{{Infobox_gene}} 
'''Regulatory factor X, 6''' also known as '''DNA-binding protein RFX6''' is a [[protein]] that in humans is encoded by the ''RFX6'' [[gene]].<ref name="entrez">{{cite web | title = Entrez Gene: Regulatory factor X, 6| url = https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene?db=gene&cmd=retrieve&list_uids=222546| access-date = 2012-02-10 <!-- T10:56:40.109-08:00 --> }}</ref>

== Function ==

The nuclear protein encoded by this gene is a member of the [[regulatory factor X]] (RFX) family of [[transcription factor]]s. Studies in mice suggest that this gene is specifically required for the differentiation of [[islet cell]]s for the production of [[insulin]], but not for the differentiation of [[pancreatic polypeptide]]-producing cells. It regulates the transcription factors involved in [[beta-cell]] maturation and function, thus, restricting the expression of the beta-cell differentiation and specification genes.

== Clinical significance ==

Mutations in this gene are associated with [[Mitchell-Riley syndrome]], which is characterized by neonatal diabetes with pancreatic [[hypoplasia]], duodenal and jejunal [[atresia]], and gall bladder agenesis.<ref name="entrez">{{cite web | title = Entrez Gene: Regulatory factor X, 6| url = https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/gene?db=gene&cmd=retrieve&list_uids=222546| access-date = 2012-02-10 <!-- T10:56:40.109-08:00 --> }}</ref>
